pi_items.verified_value: Terabyte and Gigabyte are standard ISO/IEC recognized unit names with abbreviations TB and GB

SI/IEC standard decimal system: 1 Terabyte = 10^12 bytes, 1 Gigabyte = 10^9 bytes, therefore 1 TB = 1000 GB. Verified via IEC 60027-2 standard and NIST reference (https://physics.nist.gov/cuu/Units/binary.html, https://www.iec.ch/prefixes-binary-multiples)
Task parameter - arbitrary choice for process quantitative reference amount

privileged_info_summary: This task involves creating synthetic ontology entries in OpenLCA. The unit names 'Terabyte' and 'Gigabyte' are standard ISO/IEC recognized units. The conversion factor of 1000.0 is verified correct per the SI decimal standard (IEC 60027), where 1 Terabyte = 10^12 bytes and 1 Gigabyte = 10^9 bytes, yielding 1 TB = 1000 GB. Other metadata items (unit_group_name, flow_property_name, flow_name, process_name, target_amount) are task-specific naming and parameter choices with no external ground truth.
